ads.description: Up for sale is this 1995 Gottlieb Frank Thomas Big Hurt. This machine was completely gone through (recently) and is nicer than how it left the factory! The playfield was stripped down, cleaned and waxed. Old incandescent bulbs and flashers were replaced with modern LED’s in the playfield and backbox. All rubbers were replaced and coils were replaced as well. New flipper bats. New drop target stickers, new balls, New legs, bolts and leg levelers as well as new side rails were installed. Coin door was stripped and repainted. Custom inner side art installed. Cabinet art and translite are in very very nice shape for it’s age. All serial numbers matching, and only 1,985 were ever produced! This is a clean example and perfect for your man cave whether you’re a pinball collector or a baseball fanatic. ads.author: Allen Robinson
ads.title: P.O.W. Prisoners of War, Upright Arcade, SNK 1988
ads.description: Vintage Upright Arcade – P.O.W. Prisoners of War (SNK, 1988) Price: $325 Own a piece of late-80s arcade history! This is an original P.O.W. – Prisoners of War upright cabinet from SNK, released in 1988. Known as a classic scrolling fighter / beat ’em up, players battle their way through enemy compounds after escaping captivity. It’s a gritty, action-packed title that helped define the genre and remains a nostalgic favorite for collectors. Condition: - Cabinet is very old and needs a lot of love. - Boards need work and likely new caps. - Monitor powers on but the image quality is poor. - Lights come on. - No sound currently. - This is a project cabinet — ideal for restoration enthusiasts. Highlights: - Rare SNK beat ’em up from the golden age of arcades. - Upright cabinet with authentic late-80s design. - Great candidate for restoration or display in a retro collection. Important: - Sold as-is. - Serious buyers only. No I don’t deliver but it can be picked up or shipped (arranged by buyer) in Powder Springs, GA.

ads.author: Allen Robinson
ads.title: Pole Position, Upright Arcade, Atari/Namco 1982
ads.description: Vintage Upright Arcade – Pole Position (Atari/Namco, November 1982) Price: $400 Step into racing history with this original Pole Position upright cabinet — one of the most iconic arcade racers ever made. Released in 1982 by Atari/Namco, Pole Position was a groundbreaking driving simulator that set the standard for racing games. It’s widely recognized as one of the Top 50 most historically important video games of all time. Condition: - Cabinet is very old and needs some love. - Boards need work and likely new caps. - Monitor does not power on. - Lights come on. - No sound currently. - This is a project cabinet — perfect for restoration enthusiasts or collectors. Highlights: - Classic upright racer with simulator-style gameplay. - Rare piece of arcade history from the golden age. - Great candidate for restoration or display in a collection. Important: - Price is negotiable, but only slightly. - Sold as-is. - Serious buyers only. No I don’t deliver but it can be picked up or shipped (arranged by buyer) in Powder Springs, GA.

ads.author: Allen Robinson
ads.title: Double Axle, Upright Arcade, Taitio 1990
ads.description: Vintage Upright Arcade – Double Axle (Taito, 1990) Price: $375 Bring home a piece of arcade racing history! This is an original Double Axle upright cabinet from Taito (1990). Known for its rugged off-road truck racing gameplay, Double Axle puts players behind the wheel of massive monster trucks, battling through dirt tracks, jumps, and obstacles. It’s a rare find that captures the excitement of 90s arcade culture. Condition: - Cabinet is original and shows its age — it needs some love. - Monitor has been converted to LCD. - Lights power on. - Sound is present. - Game plays and works! Highlights: - Classic Taito racing arcade with unique monster truck gameplay. - LCD conversion makes it easier to maintain. - Great candidate for restoration or display in a collection. - A working cabinet from 1990 — not something you see every day. Important: - Price is negotiable, but only slightly. - Sold as-is. - Serious buyers only. No I don’t deliver but it can be picked up or shipped (arranged by buyer) in Powder Springs, GA.

ads.author: Allen Robinson
ads.title: Capcom Bowling, Upright Arcade, Capcom 1988
ads.description: Vintage Upright Arcade – Capcom Bowling (Capcom, 1988) Price: $350 Step back into the late 80s with this original Capcom Bowling upright cabinet. Released in 1988, this sports arcade brought the fun of bowling into the arcade scene, complete with competitive play and classic retro charm. It’s a great piece for collectors or anyone looking to add a unique game to their lineup. Condition: • Cabinet is very old and needs some love. • Monitor powers on, image is good, but probably needs new caps for long-term reliability. • Lights come on. • Sound works. • Controls are functional. • Sold as-is. Highlights: • Classic Capcom sports arcade with bowling gameplay. • Upright cabinet design from the golden age of arcades. • Fully playable — monitor, sound, lights, and controls all working. • Great candidate for restoration or display in a retro collection. Important: • Serious buyers only.

ads.author: Allen Robinson
ads.title: Police Trainer, Upright Arcade, P&P Marketing 1996
ads.description: Vintage Upright Arcade – Police Trainer (P&P Marketing, 1996) Price: $425 (Negotiable, but not by much) Test your reflexes and accuracy with this original Police Trainer upright arcade cabinet from 1996. Developed by P&P Marketing, Police Trainer is a fast-paced shooter that puts players through a series of training exercises — from target practice to reaction drills — making it one of the most unique skill-based arcade shooters of the 90s. Condition: - Cabinet is original but needs some love due to age. - Monitor powers on, image is good, but likely needs new caps for long-term reliability. - Lights and sound both work. - Controls function, but the guns need recalibration. - Sold as-is. Highlights: - Classic 90s shooter with training-style gameplay. - Upright cabinet design with dual light guns. - Fully playable — monitor, lights, and sound all working. - Great candidate for restoration or display in a retro collection. Important: - Price is $425, negotiable but only slightly. - Serious buyers only. No I don’t deliver but it can be picked up or shipped (arranged by buyer) in Powder Springs, GA.

ads.author: Brett Halsey
ads.title: 1979 Flash Pinball For Sale
ads.description: Up for sale is a 1979 Flash pin ball by Williams and it’s located in Gainesville, GA. I’m selling this game as a working project because the sound isn’t working. The sound board probably needs to be recapped. You can also buy a new sound board from nvram.weebly.com for $100. Pinside has this title at $1110-$1290. I’m asking $750 working as is. My pinside profile is https://pinside.com/pinball/community/pinsiders/bretthalsey, and has nothing but positive feedback. I took dozens of pictures. If you want to see those, you can reach me directly at six, seven, eight, four, four, seven, eight, three, five six. Shipping? I prefer a local pickup, but will consider shipping if the buyer makes those arrangements.

It’s in gear condition and quite rare, and working fine. I love the machine and it’s a fun board, but I bought this in Vegas when I had a room for it. Price is firm. Local pickup only and I can help load.

ads.author: Aldo Rodrigo
ads.title: STAR TREK Pro Pinball Machine
ads.description: An excellent condition Star Trek Pro for sale. HUO - Documented (Home used only). I’m the only owner, bought NIB and I take meticulous care of my pin. Pin come from a pet and smoke free home. Low lifetime plays. A lot of professional mods installed: Vengeance Led Light Vengeance Visor Asteroid Belt Asteroid Ramp Bird of Prey Ship§ Enterprise Ship Mirror Blades Honey Comb Movie Cards Button Protectors NCC-1701 Metal Plate Speakers Icon Enterprise Topper Planet Topper Led Light Topper Back/Bottom Led Lights Warp Light Below Enterprise Keychain Inspired by the Star Trek and Star Trek Into Darkness films, the newest Stern machine puts players in Captain James Kirk's chair of the U.S.S. Enterprise, embarking on a journey to protect and defend the planets of the Federation. Stern's Star Trek pinball machine features all the bells and whistles a Star Trek fan would desire. With adrenalin-filled battles, high-definition artwork, cosmic LED lighting and classic musical scores, the Star Trek game will evoke the sensation of flying through space at Warp Speed to rescue Spock and defeat the U.S.S. Vengeance. The newest game from Stern includes an animated battleship, three lightning fast ramps, three flippers, resettable memory drop targets and much more. Star Trek is Stern's first all-LED Pro Model. The playfield features seven multicolored LED paths that lead gamers on easy-to-understand color-coded routes. There are six missions for casual players matched by 18 complex treks to immerse enthusiast players deeper in the game. As players attack an oncoming threat with three action-centered flippers, the all-LED playfield will captivate onlookers and enhance the player experience with features like phaser flashes and photon torpedoes. Players will instinctively know exactly what to do when they approach Star Trek pinball – destroy the U.S.S. Vengeance, a key action-packed feature on the playing field. From the very start, players will have the chance to unlock special opportunities, collect tantalizing bonus features and win a series of accolades called The Captain's Chair. Star Trek aficionados can expect the classic battle between the heroic Enterprise team and the villainous Khan as well as run-ins with Nero, Klingons and the Narada.

It arrived with some playfield damage that the owner of Pinball Adventures said he'd take care of. Sadly, Andrew McBain never did honor his promises to send a new playfield. So, the game still has the damage. They were supposed to make 10 of these engraved editions but I think they only made 1 or 2. It might be one of the rarest games on pinside. If you look at the engraved edition you will see only 1 person has this game in their collection. I don't think there's another pinball game on pinside that has only 1 owner. If you want to own a game no one else has, this is it!

I am the original owner, and the pin has lived exclusively in a climate-controlled, non-smoking home. It looks brand new — cabinet, playfield, armor, glass, art blades, mechs, and electronics are all in flawless condition. Running the latest Stern code (v0.89.0) and plays perfectly. Individually numbered Limited Edition: #231 / 932 Custom “Myth of Terror Island” LE art package Mirrored backglass Upgraded LE armor + powder coat Inside art blades High-end speakers + upgraded sound package LE plaque and certificate Anti-reflective HD glass Shaker motor Full RGB lighting effects Sculpted features + enhanced playfield detailing LE-exclusive trim and ornamental touches Pickup / Shipping Easy ground-level pickup Willing to coordinate shipping through STI/Beltmann, Fast Eddie, or your preferred transporter

ads.author: Tom Grizzard
ads.title: 1978 Bally PLAYBOY pinball machine -New Playfield, new cabinet decals
ads.description: Experience nostalgia with this classic 1978 Bally PLAYBOY pinball machine. This machine has been given a new lease of life with a brand new playfield and cabinet decals, making it the perfect addition to any arcade or pinball collection. The machine boasts the iconic PLAYBOY branding and provides endless hours of entertainment for all ages. Don't miss out on the chance to own this timeless piece of gaming history. Notable Features: Flippers (2), Pop bumpers (3), Slingshots (2), Standup targets (6), 5-bank drop targets (1), Star rollover (1), Upper left kickback lane (the Grotto). The backglass depicts Hugh Hefner with 1976 Playmate Patti McGuire and 1977 Playmate Sondra Theodore. Tom Nieman, VP of Marketing for Bally Pinball, told us his name or initials appeared on most backglasses of this time period for which he obtained the license. On this game, his name appears on the champagne bottle. The start-up tune, Grotto tune, and bonus score countdown tune resemble portions of Cy Coleman's "Playboy's Theme" from the 1969 TV show "Playboy After Dark". An article in the Spring 2019 issue of Playboy magazine about the "Singing Playmates" mentioned that their producer Vic Caesar "arranged the surprisingly famous theme to the Playboy pinball game." Good condition. Everything works. Will ship via freight carrier at buyer's expense... contact for quote. Local delivery available for a nominal fee.

The MPU and PPB have been professionally rebuilt and future proofed. The power supply is new. It has a Pinsound custom sound card and a large format Color DMD. Everything on the playfield is new, the plastics, the ramp, the pop bumpers, the boat. All of it. Everything under the playfield is either new or has been completely refurbished. If you know anything about me, you know I take great pride in my work. This is no exception. Is it cheap? ads.author: Jennifer Melvin Otten
ads.title: Williams “Big Guns” Pinball Machine - 1987
ads.description: The "Big Guns" pinball machine, released by Williams in 1987, was designed by Mark Ritchie and Python Anghelo and features a unique medieval-space theme. It is known for its tall backbox with a bagatelle mini-game, a two-tiered playfield, and innovative features like catapults that launch the ball, multiple ball savers, and four flippers. The game's objective is to rescue a queen from a castle by using the "big guns". Key historical and design details Manufacturer and Release: The machine was produced by Williams Electronics Games, Inc. in October 1987. Designers: Mark Ritchie designed the playfield, while Python Anghelo handled much of the artwork and overall design concept. Theme: The game blends a medieval setting with science fiction, featuring a king holding a queen hostage in a castle that is attacked by futuristic military forces. Backbox and Bagatelle: The backbox is taller than average to accommodate the "King's Chamber," a bagatelle-style mini-game where players can win bonuses or an extra ball by skillfully launching a ball into it. Innovations: Catapults: Two catapults on the playfield are used to launch the ball into the upper playfield or a habitrail. Four Flippers: The machine has four flippers, including two on the main playfield and two on an upper playfield, adding a unique two-level gameplay experience. Ball Savers: To make the game less frustrating, it includes multiple anti-frustration features like a kickback, a center pop-up post, and an outlane diverter. Recognition: "Big Guns" was well-received at the time, with Play Meter magazine calling it the best pinball machine at the 1987 AMOA show. All components of the game works including the ball on the scoreboard. All art work is in great condition. More video available upon request. Local pickup only

ads.author: Brodie Pugh
ads.title: Top Racer Arcade Board
ads.description: Untested and assumed not working This is a pole position bootleg board. It is a 3 stack. I believe the top one is the sound board, middle is the cpu bd, and bottom is video bd. The EPROMs on the sound bd have broken and corroded legs. I tried to test a couple that had exposed windows and they did not verify against the good rom. The good thing is the roms are available online for this board and a pdf of the schematic for power and video hookup. The only thing I’ve done is clean it and remove a leaky save battery. The leaky battery caused corrosion on the middle board. As far as I can tell it appears to be complete. I’m unsure if this could be fixed or not, so this is sold as is. It may be good to take chips from. $50 obo. Also open to trades for other arcade boards

ads.author: Benjamin Cook
ads.title: 1996 Sega Wave Runner Arcade Machine
ads.description: *If it's up, it's available. Local pickup near Boiling Springs SC. Electronic payments (Cash app, venmo, zelle etc) are not accepted. Cash only please* I had planned on restoring this arcade machine but life got in the way. It powers on and works to an extent but it needs TLC to get it fully functional. The picture convergence needs help. The jet ski needs an air compressor to make the ride functional which is missing so I never could test it. There's also a piece of aluminum trim on the left side of the jet ski that was missing when I got it. The Sega Model 2 inside has been replaced so the old one is included. You will need to bring a trailer with a ramp to load it. Both pieces are on wheels so it's relatively easy to move but very heavy.

ads.author: Blake Williams
ads.title: Pulp Fiction LE
ads.description: Low plays excellent shape Looking to trade or sell. The Pulp Fiction LE Pinball Machine is a limited-edition, officially licensed pinball game from Chicago Gaming Company that pays tribute to Quentin Tarantino’s cult classic film. It blends retro-inspired 1990s pinball aesthetics with modern features and deep gameplay, all themed around the movie’s iconic characters, quotes, and soundtrack. This “Bad Mother Flipper” Limited Edition machine includes exclusive touches like a numbered plaque, custom Jack Rabbit Slim’s topper, vintage-style art, interior mirror blades, shaker motor, and enhanced LED lighting. The playfield is designed by legendary pinball designer Mark Ritchie and packed with thematic elements such as interactive briefcase toys, sculpted character figures, dynamic lighting, and cinematic callouts. Audio and visuals draw you into the Pulp Fiction world with 250+ legendary lines from the film’s cast, five licensed songs from the soundtrack, and custom compositions throughout gameplay. Only 1,000 LE units were produced, making it a collectible centerpiece for fans and pinball and film lover. TRADE OR SELL

ads.title: 1974 Gottlieb Top Card pinball machine
ads.description: Up for sale is a beautiful 1974 Gottlieb Top Card pinball machine. I am listing this machine, along with another pitch and bat machine for a friend who is not on social media. This game has undergone a ground up restoration, with the cabinet being repainted using professional stencils back to its original graphics. The game has been cleaned and restored mechanically and plays great! The playfield is in great condition, as is the back glass. This game was originally purchased from an operator who removed it from his route back in the day, after only a few months, due to someone damaging the front door in an attempt to break it. The game only has 13,xxx plays on it, due to being in storage for 50 years. The game has a new reproduction front door, legs, and many other parts. It is a beautiful game which must be seen in person and played. If interested, please contact me and I can put you in touch with the owner for any arrangements. Asking $1,900 OBO. Cash only. Pick up in Flagler Beach, FL.

ads.author: Rob LaDue
ads.title: Black Rose Pinball Machine - Pirate Themed
ads.description: Gorgeous Black Rose Pinball Machine -Pirate Themed This beautiful Black Rose, pirate-themed, pinball machine was produced in 1992 and features many unique game elements, including a pop-up twisting cannon shot to sink ships and shoot bonus shots, video-mode bonus games (uses the flippers to play a Knife Throwing, Walk-the-Plank, and Swing-from-the-Rigging analog video game), Easter-egg bonuses for trick shots, treasure bonuses, parrot bonus, etc. Play is flawless! All plastics and metals are in great condition, and the lights, cannon, analog display, 3 flippers, plunger, sound, and ball eject perform perfectly. Complete Operations Manual included. This machine has been lovingly used for home play since our purchase of it in 2008. We are a smoke free home. At the time of purchase, the machine had been completely shopped, including replacing many of the older incandescent lighting with LEDs. Some of the incandescent lights were retained to provide some of that old school pinball feel. All rubbers were replaced last year, a pinball specialist gave it a once over in December 2024, and the field was recently rewaxed. The cabinet and playfield are near flawless and the legs are free of rust. Before being returned to the States for its reshop in 2007, this machine was exported to Italy. It retains its original Italian lire coin return buttons. The game speaks and displays English (go figure!). The coin change box was missing when we acquired it, but is easily found on the internet. For an older (and more affordable) pinball game, Black Rose has so-ooo many different and unique elements and shots, that our family never got tired of it (our moving necessitates the sale). The pirate theme is exciting for both adults and children too and for those that desire, the game can be upgraded with the Skull and Bones kit, which changes the game in many interesting ways. Please message me with any questions and I will do the best I can to answer them. Play tests can also be arranged (located in Bradenton, FL). Shipping must be arranged by the buyer. Beautiful machine! Thanks!

ads.author: Eric Fisher
ads.title: (REDUCED) Williams EARTHSHAKER pinball machine
ads.description: The "Earthshaker Institute", with nine lights showing all the zones you have visited. Splitting California/Nevada ramp diverter. The "Earthshaker Institute", the building in the back with the lit numbers 1-9 on it, was originally designed to move above and below the playfield. Due to the cost-reduction process, this motor assembly was left out and only the first 200 machines were produced with a "sinking building". Earthshaker is the first game to use a "shaker motor". Williams unfortunately never got down to registering a patent for the motor. The result was the widespread use of shaker motors in various pinball machines for then-rival Data East. Earthshaker is the first game with a "family" game ROM. This was done so that a word deemed offensive in the 1980's - "Bitchin'!", could be removed. About the first hundred games had a moving number square. It cost too much so they removed it from all other games. Delivery available in Florida. Game looks fantastic in person with bright all original cabinet art and mirrored back glass. All original circuit boards in the back box with no mods at all

ads.author: Brad Smith
ads.title: Used Good Flintstones Pinball Machine
ads.description: Has dinosaur, manual, CD, spare bulbs. Missing some decorative elements. The 1994 The Flintstones pinball machine, manufactured by Williams Electronic Games, is a highly regarded, family-friendly game based on the live-action movie of the same year. Known for its unique "smart ramps" that control ball flow, a dinosaur head bash toy, and voice work from the film's cast, it is a sought-after, fun, and fast-paced game. Key Features and Details: Manufacturer: Williams (1994). Production: Approx. 4,779 units produced. Features: A distinctive dinosaur head atop the playfield, a 3-target bowling alley, and "smart ramps". Audio/Voices: Features speech from John Goodman, Rick Moranis, and Harvey Korman, along with the classic theme song. Designers: Game design by John Trudeau, artwork by Kevin O'Connor. This pinball machine is not to be confused with the smaller, earlier, or handheld Flintstones toy pinball games. It is a full-sized, WPC-system machine often praised for its deep rule set and colorful, nostalgic theme.

ads.author: Richard Redd
ads.title: (RARE) Gottlieb Nudge-It Upright Arcade / Pinball Game, Circa 1990
ads.description: This is an original Nudge-It upright arcade pinball-style game produced by Gottlieb (Premier Technology) around 1990. Nudge-It Is a distinctive and unconventional entry ir Gottlied's late-era catalog, designed without traditional flippers. Instead, gameplay relies on a spring-loaded plunger and controlled cabinet nudging to guide the ball into illuminated scoring lanes, creating a hybric experience between pinball and skill-based arcade play. From a historical perspective, Nudge-It represents one of Gottlieb's experimental designs during the final years of large-scale American pinball manufacturing. Unlike standard pinball machines, the absence of flippers was intentional, encouraging physical interaction and timing rather than reflex-based flipper control. This design makes Nudge-It stand out among collectors as a novelty machine with strong visual appeal and an approachable learning curve for casual players. Production & Rarity: Exact production figures for Nudge-It were not formally published, but collector consensus and industry references generally estimate production at approximately 2,000 units or fewer, making it a relatively low-production title compared to mainstream pinball machines of the era. As a result, surviving examples-particularly complete, powered-on machines-are considered uncommon on today's secondary market.This electrical standing arcade game by Gottlieb stands 22" X 37" X 57" high at the back. The spring-attached knob releases the silver chrome ball perfectly. No flappers were designed for this arcade game. The idea was to "nudge" the ball into the lit lane. This electrical standing arcade game measures approximately 22 inches wide by 37 inches deep and 57 inches high at the back. The spring-attached plunger releases the chrome ball smoothly, and the illuminated playfield elements activate as designed. No flippers were manufactured for this game, as nudging the cabinet is the intended method of ball control. The cabinet, backglass, and playfield artwork display expected cosmetic wear consistent with age and commercial use.

Bought new pop caps, new clear plastic protectors, power supply cap kit, orginal backglass was broken so I got a new translite and glass (glass and translite are to thicker than orginal channels. will need to replace them or get a thinner piece of glass I got 1/4 inch glass) A couple displays where not perfect might be the power supply or connectors also. Moving the displays around got them to work in different locations if I remember correctly. Left flipper coil is burned up, I might have a used one I could toss in. That's where I stopped didn't look at the driver board so test the transistor for it. ads.author: Rick Hampton
ads.title: Arcade Games for sale
ads.description: I have 13 Video games for sale as a lot. I AM NOT SELLING INDIVIDUALLY. The price is firm. It comes out to be 250.00 per game and I will include a Winners Wheel redemption game for free. Most of these games worked when I moved them from my former home to my new home. I will list the games and what I know about them now. After powering them up for the first time sense the move I find that most of them have one problem or another. It could be just a loose board from the move or power supply needs to be adjusted. You are welcome to check them out yourself if you are interested in them. 1. Super GT. There are 2 of theses with the marquee topper. I believe both of these are working. 2. Hydro Thunder. was working perfectly but now game plays but no picture. Comes with the seat 3. Off Road Challenge. There are 2 of these with the marquee topper. Both worked when moved. Both play but picture problems. They are set up to play together .One is the master and the other ids the slave. They aren't connected right now and that could be the problem. 4. Big Buck Hunter World. Played perfectly until the move .Game plays but no picture. 5. Silver Strike Bowling. When I purchased it I was told all it needed was a monitor. I have a new monitor for it but haven't put it on. I have never tested this game. I moved a few days after buying it. 6. Target Terror. Worked with no problems before move. 7. Ranger Mission. Worked with no problems .Now it doesn't have a picture. I also broke the plastic front screen cover as you can see in the picture of it. 8. Virtua Cop 2. Just needs guns calibrated. 9.Dream Arcade, LLC Vision 29 Multicade Game . Was working with no problems. It uses a Dell computer and has a an error when booting up. I have several additional CDs with a lot of games other than the ones already loaded on it. Dream Arcade LLC currently sells the Vision 29 for over 3000.00. 10.Golden Tee Fore. Has missing parts. There is one board and a couple hard drives there but I believe one board is missing. Its another game I purchased and never tested, 11.Ghost Squad. When I purchased it I was told all it needed was a replacement hard drive which came with it. I have never had the time to check it out, so I don't know the status of it. Please don't waste my time if your not serious about buying them. I can send additional pictures if you are seriously interested. If you have no experience with these type games you can get help on Facebook groups that are very experienced in repairing them. Odds are there is one of them in your area. I am in Santee Sc. DON,T ASK IS IT AVAILABLE. If its listed its available.
